revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: 札幌第3の神社。連合用水メモリアル関連が多い
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=豊平神社| 画像=[[File:Toyohira Shrine.JPG|260px]]| 所在地=北海道札幌市豊平区豊平4条13丁目1番18号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|2|39.1|N|141|25|51|E|scale:25000}}| 祭神=[[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]| 創建=[[1871年]]（[[明治]]4年）| 本殿=神明造| 社格=郷社| 例祭=7月15日| 別名= }} '''豊平神社'''（とよひらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]]豊平4条13丁目1番18号にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。
== 祭神 == ;[[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]（かみつけのたみちのみこと） :主祭神。[[阿部仁太郎]]が故郷・青森の[[猿賀神社]]の神を入植先の当地でも祭ったもの<ref name="jisha">「第2章 神社の形成と歩み - 豊平区」『札幌の寺社』</ref>。 ;[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]（おおやまつみのかみ） :山の神。入植当時は一帯が森林で、毎年多くの木材を切り出していたことから祭られた<ref name="jisha" />。 ;[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） :実りの神。田畑を開墾するにあたって祭られた<ref name="jisha" />。
== 歴史 == *[[1871年]]（明治4年） - 阿部仁太郎が上毛野田道命の祠を建てる<ref name="jisha" />。 *[[1884年]]（明治17年） - 社殿を新築<ref name="jisha" />。 *[[1889年]]（明治22年） - 公認神社となる<ref name="jisha" />。 *[[1919年]]（大正8年）1月 - 村社となる<ref name="jisha" />。 *[[1943年]]（昭和18年） - 郷社となる<ref name="jisha" />。
== 碑 == ;阿部仁太郎之碑 :[[1899年]]（明治32年）7月3日建立。豊平区に現存する最古の碑といわれる{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.1}}。 ;満州戦没記念碑 :[[1906年]]（明治39年）建立。[[日露戦争]]で犠牲となった豊平村出身者の慰霊のための碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.3}}。 ;連合用水「穿渠有功者姓名」碑 :[[1914年]]（大正5年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。豊平・平岸・上白石・白石の四カ村連合用水の開設功労者を顕彰する碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.9}}。 ;「聯合用水開渠四十周年記念国富在農」碑 :[[1933年]]（昭和8年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。題書は当時の札幌市長・[[橋本正治]]{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.17}}。 ;「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 :[[1943年]]（昭和18年）9月1日建立{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.21}}。 ;針供養の歌碑 :[[1951年]]（昭和26年）12月建立<ref name="jisha" />。 ;連合用水組合「六十周年記念功労者」碑 :[[1953年]]（昭和28年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.25}}。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|阿部仁太郎之碑 File:Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|満州戦没記念碑 File:Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 File:Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|針供養の歌碑 File:Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|交通安全祈念塔 </gallery> ==脚注== {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『札幌の寺社』[[さっぽろ文庫]]39 *{{cite journal|和書|author=中村美彦|title=豊平区の碑|journal=札幌の碑|ref={{SfnRef|中村}}}}さっぽろ文庫45 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Toyohira-jinja}} *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01003.html 豊平神社（北海道神社庁）] {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひらしんしや}} [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]]

__NOTOC__ {{神社| 名称=豊平神社| 画像=[[File:Toyohira Shrine.JPG|260px]]| 所在地=北海道札幌市豊平区豊平4条13丁目1番18号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|2|39.1|N|141|25|51|E|scale:25000}}| 祭神=[[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]| 創建=[[1871年]]（[[明治]]4年）| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 社格=[[郷社]]| 例祭=7月15日| 別名= }} '''豊平神社'''（とよひらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[豊平]]4条13丁目1番18号にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == ; [[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]（かみつけのたみちのみこと） : 主祭神。[[阿部仁太郎]]が故郷・青森の[[猿賀神社]]の神を入植先の当地でも祭ったもの「第2章 神社の形成と歩み - 豊平区」『札幌の寺社』。 ; [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]（おおやまつみのかみ） : 山の神。入植当時は一帯が森林で、毎年多くの木材を切り出していたことから祭られた。 ; [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） : 実りの神。田畑を開墾するにあたって祭られた。 == 歴史 == * [[1871年]]（明治4年） – [[阿部仁太郎]]が上毛野田道命の祠を建てる。 * [[1884年]]（明治17年） – 社殿を新築。 * [[1889年]]（明治22年） – 公認神社となる。 * [[1919年]]（大正8年）1月 – [[村社]]となる。 * [[1943年]]（昭和18年） – 郷社となる。 == 碑 == ; 阿部仁太郎之碑 : [[1899年]]（明治32年）7月3日建立。豊平区に現存する最古の碑といわれる{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.1}}。 ; 満州戦没記念碑 : [[1906年]]（明治39年）建立。[[日露戦争]]で犠牲となった[[豊平村]]出身者の慰霊のための碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.3}}。 ; 連合用水「穿渠有功者姓名」碑 : [[1914年]]（大正5年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。豊平・[[平岸村]]・[[上白石村]]・[[白石村 (北海道)|白石村]]の四カ村連合用水の開設功労者を顕彰する碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.9}}。 ; 「聯合用水開渠四十周年記念国富在農」碑 : [[1933年]]（昭和8年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。題書は当時の札幌市長・[[橋本正治]]{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.17}}。 ; 「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 : [[1943年]]（昭和18年）9月1日建立{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.21}}。 ; [[針供養]]の歌碑 : [[1951年]]（昭和26年）12月建立。 ; 連合用水組合「六十周年記念功労者」碑 : [[1953年]]（昭和28年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.25}}。 == ギャラリー == File:Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|阿部仁太郎之碑 File:Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|満州戦没記念碑 File:Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 File:Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|針供養の歌碑 File:Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|交通安全祈念塔 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『札幌の寺社』[[さっぽろ文庫]]39 * {{cite journal|和書|author=[[中村美彦]]|title=豊平区の碑|journal=札幌の碑|ref={{SfnRef|中村}}}}さっぽろ文庫45 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Toyohira-jinja}} * [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ 豊平神社] *: [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/%E6%8E%88%E4%B8%8E%E5%93%81-2/ [[授与品]]] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01003.html 豊平神社（[[北海道神社庁]]）] {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひらしんしや}} [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]]

== 碑 == ; 阿部仁太郎之碑 : [[1899年]]（明治32年）7月3日建立。豊平区に現存する最古の碑といわれる{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.1}}。 ; 満州戦没記念碑 : [[1906年]]（明治39年）建立。[[日露戦争]]で犠牲となった[[豊平村]]出身者の慰霊のための碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.3}}。 ; 連合用水「穿渠有功者姓名」碑 : [[1914年]]（大正5年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。豊平・[[平岸村]]・[[上白石村]]・[[白石村 (北海道)|白石村]]の四カ村連合用水の開設功労者を顕彰する碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.9}}。 ; 「聯合用水開渠四十周年記念国富在農」碑 : [[1933年]]（昭和8年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。題書は当時の札幌市長・[[橋本正治]]{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.17}}。 ; 「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 : [[1943年]]（昭和18年）9月1日建立{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.21}}。 ; [[針供養]]の歌碑 : [[1951年]]（昭和26年）12月建立<ref name="jisha" />。 ; 連合用水組合「六十周年記念功労者」碑 : [[1953年]]（昭和28年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.25}}。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|阿部仁太郎之碑 File:Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|満州戦没記念碑 File:Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 File:Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|針供養の歌碑 File:Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|交通安全祈念塔 </gallery> == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『札幌の寺社』[[さっぽろ文庫]]39 * {{cite journal|和書|author=[[中村美彦]]|title=豊平区の碑|journal=札幌の碑|ref={{SfnRef|中村}}}}さっぽろ文庫45 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Toyohira-jinja}} * [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ 豊平神社] *: [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/%E6%8E%88%E4%B8%8E%E5%93%81-2/ ''[[授与品]]''] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01003.html 豊平神社（[[北海道神社庁]]）] {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひらしんしや}} [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]]

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{神社| 名称=豊平神社| 画像=[[File:Toyohira Shrine.JPG|260px]]| 所在地=北海道札幌市豊平区豊平4条13丁目1番18号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|2|39.1|N|141|25|51|E|scale:25000}}| 祭神=[[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]| 創建=[[1871年]]（[[明治]]4年）| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 社格=[[郷社]]| 例祭=7月15日| 別名= }} '''豊平神社'''（とよひらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]4条13丁目1番18号にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。
== 祭神 == ; [[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]（かみつけのたみちのみこと） : 主祭神。[[阿部仁太郎]]が故郷・青森の[[猿賀神社]]の神を入植先の当地でも祭ったもの<ref name="jisha">「第2章 神社の形成と歩み - 豊平区」『札幌の寺社』</ref>。 ; [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]（おおやまつみのかみ） : 山の神。入植当時は一帯が森林で、毎年多くの木材を切り出していたことから祭られた<ref name="jisha" />。 ; [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） : 実りの神。田畑を開墾するにあたって祭られた<ref name="jisha" />。
== 歴史 == * [[1871年]]（明治4年） – [[阿部仁太郎]]が上毛野田道命の祠を建てる<ref name="jisha" />。 * [[1884年]]（明治17年） – 社殿を新築<ref name="jisha" />。 * [[1889年]]（明治22年） – 公認神社となる<ref name="jisha" />。 * [[1919年]]（大正8年）1月 – [[村社]]となる<ref name="jisha" />。 * [[1943年]]（昭和18年） – 郷社となる<ref name="jisha" />。
== 碑 == ; 阿部仁太郎之碑 : [[1899年]]（明治32年）7月3日建立。豊平区に現存する最古の碑といわれる{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.1}}。 ; 満州戦没記念碑 : [[1906年]]（明治39年）建立。[[日露戦争]]で犠牲となった[[豊平村]]出身者の慰霊のための碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.3}}。 ; 連合用水「穿渠有功者姓名」碑 : [[1914年]]（大正5年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。豊平・[[平岸村]]・[[上白石村]]・[[白石村 (北海道)|白石村]]の四カ村連合用水の開設功労者を顕彰する碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.9}}。 ; 「聯合用水開渠四十周年記念国富在農」碑 : [[1933年]]（昭和8年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。題書は当時の札幌市長・[[橋本正治]]{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.17}}。 ; 「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 : [[1943年]]（昭和18年）9月1日建立{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.21}}。 ; [[針供養]]の歌碑 : [[1951年]]（昭和26年）12月建立<ref name="jisha" />。 ; 連合用水組合「六十周年記念功労者」碑 : [[1953年]]（昭和28年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.25}}。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|阿部仁太郎之碑 File:Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|満州戦没記念碑 File:Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 File:Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|針供養の歌碑 File:Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|交通安全祈念塔 </gallery> == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『札幌の寺社』[[さっぽろ文庫]]39 * {{cite journal|和書|author=[[中村美彦]]|title=豊平区の碑|journal=札幌の碑|ref={{SfnRef|中村}}}}さっぽろ文庫45 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Toyohira-jinja}} * [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ 豊平神社] *: [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/%E6%8E%88%E4%B8%8E%E5%93%81-2/ ''[[授与品]]''] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01003.html 豊平神社（[[北海道神社庁]]）] {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひらしんしや}} [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

__NOTOC__ {{神社 |名称=豊平神社 |画像=[[File:Toyohira Shrine.JPG|260px]] |所在地=北海道札幌市豊平区豊平4条13丁目1番18号 | 緯度度 = 43 | 緯度分 = 2 | 緯度秒 = 39.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 141 |経度分 = 22 | 経度秒 = 50.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Sapporo2 |祭神=[[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |創建=[[1871年]]（[[明治]]4年） |本殿=[[神明造]] |社格=[[郷社]] |例祭=7月15日 |別名= }} '''豊平神社'''（とよひらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]4条13丁目1番18号にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。

== 碑 == ; 阿部仁太郎之碑 : [[1899年]]（明治32年）7月3日建立。豊平区に現存する最古の碑といわれる{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.1}}。 ; 満州戦没記念碑 : [[1906年]]（明治39年）建立。[[日露戦争]]で犠牲となった[[豊平村]]出身者の慰霊のための碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.3}}。 ; 連合用水「穿渠有功者姓名」碑 : [[1914年]]（大正5年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。豊平・[[平岸村]]・[[上白石村]]・[[白石村 (北海道)|白石村]]の四カ村連合用水の開設功労者を顕彰する碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.9}}。 ; 「聯合用水開渠四十周年記念国富在農」碑 : [[1933年]]（昭和8年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。題書は当時の札幌市長・[[橋本正治]]{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.17}}。 ; 「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 : [[1943年]]（昭和18年）9月1日建立{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.21}}。 ; [[針供養]]の歌碑 : [[1951年]]（昭和26年）12月建立<ref name="jisha" />。 ; 連合用水組合「六十周年記念功労者」碑 : [[1953年]]（昭和28年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.25}}。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|阿部仁太郎之碑 File:Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|満州戦没記念碑 File:Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 File:Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|針供養の歌碑 File:Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|交通安全祈念塔 </gallery> == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『札幌の寺社』[[さっぽろ文庫]]39 * {{cite journal|和書|author=[[中村美彦]]|title=豊平区の碑|journal=札幌の碑|ref={{SfnRef|中村}}}}さっぽろ文庫45 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Toyohira-jinja}} * [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ 豊平神社] *: [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/%E6%8E%88%E4%B8%8E%E5%93%81-2/ ''[[授与品]]''] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01003.html 豊平神社（[[北海道神社庁]]）] {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひらしんしや}} [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{神社 |名称=豊平神社 |画像=[[File:Toyohira Shrine.JPG|260px]] |所在地=北海道札幌市豊平区豊平4条13丁目1番18号 | 緯度度 = 43 | 緯度分 = 2 | 緯度秒 = 39.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 141 |経度分 = 22 | 経度秒 = 50.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Sapporo2 |祭神=[[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |創建=[[1871年]]（[[明治]]4年） |本殿=[[神明造]] |社格=[[郷社]] |例祭=7月15日 |別名= }} '''豊平神社'''（とよひらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]4条13丁目1番18号にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。
== 祭神 == ; [[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]（かみつけのたみちのみこと） : 主祭神。[[阿部仁太郎]]が故郷・青森の[[猿賀神社]]の神を入植先の当地でも祭ったもの<ref name="jisha">「第2章 神社の形成と歩み - 豊平区」『札幌の寺社』</ref>。 ; [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]（おおやまつみのかみ） : 山の神。入植当時は一帯が森林で、毎年多くの木材を切り出していたことから祭られた<ref name="jisha" />。 ; [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） : 実りの神。田畑を開墾するにあたって祭られた<ref name="jisha" />。
== 歴史 == * [[1871年]]（明治4年） – [[阿部仁太郎]]が上毛野田道命の祠を建てる<ref name="jisha" />。 * [[1884年]]（明治17年） – 社殿を新築<ref name="jisha" />。 * [[1889年]]（明治22年） – 公認神社となる<ref name="jisha" />。 * [[1919年]]（大正8年）1月 – [[村社]]となる<ref name="jisha" />。 * [[1943年]]（昭和18年） – 郷社となる<ref name="jisha" />。
== 碑 == ; 阿部仁太郎之碑 : [[1899年]]（明治32年）7月3日建立。豊平区に現存する最古の碑といわれる{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.1}}。 ; 満州戦没記念碑 : [[1906年]]（明治39年）建立。[[日露戦争]]で犠牲となった[[豊平村]]出身者の慰霊のための碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.3}}。 ; 連合用水「穿渠有功者姓名」碑 : [[1914年]]（大正5年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。豊平・[[平岸村]]・[[上白石村]]・[[白石村 (北海道)|白石村]]の四カ村連合用水の開設功労者を顕彰する碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.9}}。 ; 「聯合用水開渠四十周年記念国富在農」碑 : [[1933年]]（昭和8年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。題書は当時の札幌市長・[[橋本正治]]{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.17}}。 ; 「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 : [[1943年]]（昭和18年）9月1日建立{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.21}}。 ; [[針供養]]の歌碑 : [[1951年]]（昭和26年）12月建立<ref name="jisha" />。 ; 連合用水組合「六十周年記念功労者」碑 : [[1953年]]（昭和28年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.25}}。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|阿部仁太郎之碑 File:Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|満州戦没記念碑 File:Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 File:Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|針供養の歌碑 File:Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|交通安全祈念塔 </gallery> == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『札幌の寺社』[[さっぽろ文庫]]39 * {{cite journal|和書|author=[[中村美彦]]|title=豊平区の碑|journal=札幌の碑|ref={{SfnRef|中村}}}}さっぽろ文庫45 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Toyohira-jinja}} * [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ 豊平神社] *: [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/%E6%8E%88%E4%B8%8E%E5%93%81-2/ ''[[授与品]]''] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01003.html 豊平神社（北海道神社庁）] {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひらしんしや}} [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=豊平神社 |画像=[[File:Toyohira Shrine.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地=北海道札幌市豊平区豊平4条13丁目1番18号 | 緯度度 = 43 | 緯度分 = 2 | 緯度秒 = 39.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 141 |経度分 = 22 | 経度秒 = 50.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Sapporo2 |祭神=[[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |創建=[[1871年]]（[[明治]]4年） |本殿=[[神明造]] |社格=旧[[郷社]] |例祭=7月15日 |別名= }} '''豊平神社'''（とよひらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]4条13丁目1番18号にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == ; [[上毛野田道|上毛野田道命]]（かみつけのたみちのみこと） : 主祭神。[[阿部仁太郎]]が故郷・青森の[[猿賀神社]]の神を入植先の当地でも祭ったもの「第2章 神社の形成と歩み - 豊平区」『札幌の寺社』。 ; [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]（おおやまつみのかみ） : 山の神。入植当時は一帯が森林で、毎年多くの木材を切り出していたことから祭られた。 ; [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） : 実りの神。田畑を開墾するにあたって祭られた。 == 歴史 == * [[1871年]]（明治4年） – [[阿部仁太郎]]が上毛野田道命の祠を建てる。 * [[1884年]]（明治17年） – 社殿を新築。 * [[1889年]]（明治22年） – 公認神社となる。 * [[1919年]]（大正8年）1月 – [[村社]]となる。 * [[1943年]]（昭和18年） – 郷社となる。 == 石碑 == ; 阿部仁太郎之碑 : [[1899年]]（明治32年）7月3日建立。豊平区に現存する最古の碑といわれる{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.1}}。 ; 満州戦没記念碑 : [[1906年]]（明治39年）建立。[[日露戦争]]で犠牲となった[[豊平村]]出身者の慰霊のための碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.3}}。 ; 連合用水「穿渠有功者姓名」碑 : [[1914年]]（大正5年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。豊平・[[平岸村]]・[[上白石村]]・[[白石村 (北海道)|白石村]]の四カ村連合用水の開設功労者を顕彰する碑{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.9}}。 ; 「聯合用水開渠四十周年記念国富在農」碑 : [[1933年]]（昭和8年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。題書は当時の札幌市長・[[橋本正治]]{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.17}}。 ; 「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 : [[1943年]]（昭和18年）9月1日建立{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.21}}。 ; [[針供養]]の歌碑 : [[1951年]]（昭和26年）12月建立。 ; 連合用水組合「六十周年記念功労者」碑 : [[1953年]]（昭和28年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在{{Sfn|中村|loc=No.25}}。 == ギャラリー == File:Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|阿部仁太郎之碑 File:Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|満州戦没記念碑 File:Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 File:Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|針供養の歌碑 File:Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|交通安全祈念塔 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『札幌の寺社』[[さっぽろ文庫]]39 * {{cite journal|和書|author=[[中村美彦]]|title=豊平区の碑|journal=札幌の碑|ref={{SfnRef|中村}}}}さっぽろ文庫45 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Toyohira-jinja}} * [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ 豊平神社] *: [http://toyohirajinja.or.jp/%E6%8E%88%E4%B8%8E%E5%93%81-2/ ''[[授与品]]''] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01003.html 豊平神社（北海道神社庁）] {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひらしんしや}} [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

阿部仁太郎之碑 : [[1899年]]（明治32年）7月3日建立。豊平区に現存する最古の碑といわれる{{Sfn|中村|1988|loc=No.1}}。 ; 満州戦没記念碑 : [[1906年]]（明治39年）建立。[[日露戦争]]で犠牲となった[[豊平町 (北海道)#歴史|豊平村]]出身者の慰霊のための碑{{Sfn|中村|1988|loc=No.3}}。 ; 連合用水「穿渠有功者姓名」碑 : [[1914年]]（大正5年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。豊平・[[平岸村]]・[[上白石村]]・[[白石村 (北海道)|白石村]]の四カ村連合用水の開設功労者を顕彰する碑{{Sfn|中村|1988|loc=No.9}}。 ; 「聯合用水開渠四十周年記念国富在農」碑 : [[1933年]]（昭和8年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在。題書は当時の札幌市長・[[橋本正治]]{{Sfn|中村|1988|loc=No.17}}。 ; 「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 : [[1943年]]（昭和18年）9月1日建立{{Sfn|中村|1988|loc=No.21}}。 ; [[針供養]]の歌碑 : [[1951年]]（昭和26年）12月建立{{Sfn|札幌市教育委員会文化資料室|1986|p=}}。 ; 連合用水組合「六十周年記念功労者」碑 : [[1953年]]（昭和28年）9月1日建立。社務所の中庭に所在{{Sfn|中村|1988|loc=No.25}}。 == ギャラリー == Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|阿部仁太郎之碑 Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|満州戦没記念碑 Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|「札幌市豊平聯合用水組合創立五十年記念」碑 Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|針供養の歌碑 Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|交通安全祈念塔 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=札幌市教育委員会文化資料室 |title=札幌の寺社 |publisher=[[北海道新聞社]] |series=[[さっぽろ文庫]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=第2章 神社の形成と歩み - 豊平区 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=中村美彦 |authorlink=中村美彦 |title=札幌の碑 |publisher=北海道新聞社 |series=さっぽろ文庫 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=豊平区の碑 |ref={{SfnRef|中村|1988}}}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Toyohira-jinja}} * {{Official website|url=https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/|name=豊平神社}} - 公式ウェブサイト ** [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/amulet/ 授与品・御朱印] * {{Instagram|toyohira_jinja}} * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ 豊平神社] - 北海道神社庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひらしんしや}} [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧郷社]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; [[Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto]] (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument 50th Anniversary Irrigation Monument.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Needle Memorial Tanka Monument.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Traffic Safety Tower at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; [[Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto]] (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.

== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; [[Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto]] (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:dd]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:dd]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "-qq]]" to "—>[[category:qq close]]"
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[ja:T" to "[[sn:"
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Deprecated qq open]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
