revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Attempting to force refresh]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.comment: adding defaultsort to all of them
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

revision.comment: removed [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]; added [[Category:Completion tier 5]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:completion tier 2]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq 1-20 (2)]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Ōyamatsumi no Kami" to "Ōyamatsumi"
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] on (CatAlot)
revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=Sapporo City Education Committee Cultural Resources Division |title=Sapporo no Jinja |publisher=[[Hokkaido Shimbun Press]] |series=[[Sapporo Bunko]] 39 |date=1986-12 |id={{JP番号|88001399}} |chapter=Chapter 2: Formation and History of Shrines - Toyohira Ward}} * {{Cite book |author=Michihiko Nakamura |title=Sapporo Monuments |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbun Press |series=Sapporo Bunko 45 |date=1988-06 |id={{JP番号|88040461}} |chapter=Monuments in Toyohira Ward}}
== External Links == * [https://toyohirajinja.or.jp/ Toyohira Shrine Official Website] (Japanese) * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/{{urlencode|豊平神社}}/ Toyohira Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association (Japanese)

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: ; {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft}} (上毛野田道命) : The main deity. Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity. It was enshrined to honor the forestry activities during the settlement period. ; [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] (倉稲魂命) : The deity of agriculture. Enshrined to support the settlers' agricultural endeavors.
== History == * [[1871]] (Meiji 4) – [[Abe Nitaro]] constructed a small shrine to enshrine Kamitsukeno Tamichi no Mikoto. * [[1884]] (Meiji 17) – Construction of a new shrine building. * [[1889]] (Meiji 22) – Official recognition as a public shrine. * [[1919]] (Taisho 8) – Designated as a ''sonsha'' (village shrine). * [[1943]] (Showa 18) – Elevated to ''gōsha'' status.
== Stone Monuments == ; Monument to Nitaro Abe : Erected on July 3, [[1899]] (Meiji 32), it is said to be the oldest surviving monument in Toyohira Ward. ; Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument : Erected in [[1906]] (Meiji 39) to honor those from Toyohira Village who perished in the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. ; Irrigation Union "Meritorious Names" Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1914]] (Taisho 3), located in the shrine's courtyard, it commemorates those who contributed to the establishment of irrigation for Toyohira and neighboring villages. ; 50th Anniversary of the Irrigation Union Monument : Erected on September 1, [[1943]] (Showa 18), inscribed with the name of then-Mayor [[Shoji Hashimoto]] of Sapporo.
== Gallery == <gallery> Monument of Nitaro Abe.JPG|Monument to Nitaro Abe Monument to War Dead in Manchuria at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Manchuria War Dead Memorial Monument Monument to the 50th Anniversary of Irrigation Water Union.JPG|50th Anniversary Irrigation Union Monument Tanka Monument to Needles at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Tanka Monument for Needle Memorials Monument to Traffic Safety at Toyohira Shrine.JPG|Traffic Safety Memorial Tower </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

'''Toyohira Shrine''' (豊平神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan. Its former shrine rank under the [[modern shrine ranking system]] was ''Gōsha'' (郷社).

Worship began when [[Abe Nitaro]], a settler from Aomori, enshrined a deity from [[Saruka Shrine]] in his homeland. ; [[Ōyamatsumi]] (大山祇神) : The mountain deity.
